title: Producer/Consumer Cities
description: Certain cities are producers of goods and some are consumers. This fundamental distinction dictates prices - producer cities have low prices for their goods, consumer cities have high prices. This creates arbitrage opportunities that drive trade routes.
definition-of-done: Cities tagged as producer or consumer for each good type. Producer cities have lower base prices. Consumer cities have higher base prices. City production/consumption visible in UI.
testing-plan: Verify cities correctly tagged. Test price differences between producer/consumer. Verify UI shows production/consumption status.
todos: Create base price modifiers for roles
Design UI for city role display
unknowns: Can a city be both producer and consumer
